title: Here, friends, a person has no defilements but does not truly understand: 'There are no defilements within me.' For such a person, this can be…
content: Here, friends, a person has no defilements but does not truly understand: 'There are no defilements within me.' For such a person, this can be expected: 'He will give attention to the sign of the beautiful. By giving attention to the sign of the beautiful, greed will defile his mind. He will die while still having greed, still having hatred, still having delusion, still having defilements, still having a defiled mind.' Friends, just as a bronze bowl, brought from the market or from the smithy, pure and clean, and its owner does not use it, does not clean it, and throws it into a dusty place. Friends, after a time, that bowl becomes even more defiled, even dustier.

title: Bhikkhus, there are these three internal stains, internal enemies, internal opponents, internal murderers, internal adversaries. What three? Greed, bhikkhus, is an internal stain, internal enemy,…
content: Bhikkhus, there are these three internal stains, internal enemies, internal opponents, internal murderers, internal adversaries. What three? Greed, bhikkhus, is an internal stain, internal enemy, internal opponent, internal murderer, internal adversary. Hatred, bhikkhus, is an internal stain, internal enemy, internal opponent, internal murderer, internal adversary. Delusion, bhikkhus, is an internal stain, internal enemy, internal opponent, internal murderer, internal adversary. These are the three internal stains, internal enemies, internal opponents, internal murderers, internal adversaries.

title: Young householder, in five ways a gentleman should attend on samaṇas and brahmins as the upper direction: acting with loving-kindness in body, acting with loving-kindness…
content: Young householder, in five ways a gentleman should attend on samaṇas and brahmins as the upper direction: acting with loving-kindness in body, acting with loving-kindness in speech, acting with loving-kindness in mind, keeping open house for them, and supplying their material needs. Young householder, when the gentleman attends on samaṇas and brahmins as the upper direction in these five ways, the samaṇas and brahmins have compassion for him in five ways: they restrain him from evil, they encourage him to do good, they have compassion for him with a mind of loving-kindness, they teach him what he has not heard, they purify what he has heard, and they point out the path to heaven.

title: And, bhikkhus, how is a bhikkhu complete in beauty? Here, bhikkhus, a bhikkhu observes the precepts, lives restrained by the restraint of the Pātimokkha, perfect…
content:
And, bhikkhus, how is a bhikkhu complete in beauty?
Here, bhikkhus, a bhikkhu observes the precepts, lives restrained by the restraint of the Pātimokkha, perfect in conduct and resort, seeing danger in the slightest faults, he undertakes and trains in the training rules. Thus, bhikkhus, is a bhikkhu complete in beauty.

title: My mind has traveled all around, To every direction, Yet I have not found anything Dearer than myself. Since the self is so dear to…
content:
My mind has traveled all around,
To every direction,
Yet I have not found anything
Dearer than myself.

Since the self is so dear to everyone,
Therefore, one who loves oneself
Should not harm another's self.

title: Truly, this is one of the benefits, bhikkhus, namely dwelling in the forest... going for alms... wearing dust-heap robes... using only three robes... teaching the…
content: Truly, this is one of the benefits, bhikkhus, namely dwelling in the forest... going for alms... wearing dust-heap robes... using only three robes... teaching the Dhamma... being learned in the Discipline... knowing much about the truth... being an elder for a long time... having proper demeanor... having a following... being surrounded by a great assembly... coming from a good family... having a handsome appearance... having pleasant speech... having few desires... being without illness.

title: Cunda, you should assemble together in harmony and mutual respect, without disputing. A fellow celibate teaches the Dhamma in the midst of the Saṅgha. If…
content: Cunda, you should assemble together in harmony and mutual respect, without disputing. A fellow celibate teaches the Dhamma in the midst of the Saṅgha. If you think: 'This venerable one has grasped the meaning incorrectly or the phrasing incorrectly,' you should neither praise nor reject him, but should carefully explain to him both the meaning and the phrasing.

title: Friends, when a noble disciple understands suffering, understands the origin of suffering, understands the cessation of suffering, understands the path leading to the cessation of…
content: Friends, when a noble disciple understands suffering, understands the origin of suffering, understands the cessation of suffering, understands the path leading to the cessation of suffering — then, friends, that noble disciple has right view, straight view, perfect confidence in the Dhamma, and has attained this excellent Dhamma.

title: Bhikkhus, here a bhikkhu goes to the forest, to the root of a tree, or to an empty hut, and sits down, folding his legs…
content: Bhikkhus, here a bhikkhu goes to the forest, to the root of a tree, or to an empty hut, and sits down, folding his legs crosswise, keeping his body straight, and establishing mindfulness in front of him. Mindfully he breathes in, mindfully he breathes out. Breathing in long, he understands: 'I breathe in long'; or breathing out long, he understands: 'I breathe out long'; breathing in short, he understands: 'I breathe in short'; or breathing out short, he understands: 'I breathe out short'; 'Experiencing the whole body, I will breathe in,' he trains; 'Experiencing the whole body, I will breathe out,' he trains; 'Tranquilizing the bodily formation, I will breathe in,' he trains; 'Tranquilizing the bodily formation, I will breathe out,' he trains.

title: And, bhikkhus, what is the person who dwells in darkness heading toward light? Here, bhikkhus, a person is born into a low family — a…
content: And, bhikkhus, what is the person who dwells in darkness heading toward light? Here, bhikkhus, a person is born into a low family — a scavenger's family, a hunter's family, a basket-maker's family, a bamboo-worker's family, a trapper's family, a cart-maker's family, or a sweeper's family — a poor family with little food and drink, where livelihood is hard, where food and clothing are hard to find. And he is ugly, unsightly, deformed, ill, blind in one eye, crooked-limbed, lame, or half-paralyzed; he does not obtain food, drink, clothing, vehicles, garlands, scents, ointments, beds, dwellings, or lamps. He dwells doing good bodily actions, good verbal actions, good mental actions. Having done so, after death, with the breakup of the body, he is reborn in a good destination, a heavenly world. Thus, bhikkhus, is the person who dwells in darkness heading toward light.
